pending.body:
finding_summary: The new BR adapter suite never exercises the dependency mutation entry points, so one of the bead's explicit scope items (nominal dependency operations) is still unverified.
severity: low
affected_files:
- tests/unit/br_adapter_test.rs
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
evidence:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1410-1445 contains `add_dependency`, `add_dependency_with_kind`, and `remove_dependency`, each with their own command construction and tracked-mutation/journaling path.
- tests/unit/br_adapter_test.rs:281-328 covers only `dep tree` read parsing, and tests/unit/br_adapter_test.rs:360-570 moves on to create/update/close/sync without any add/remove dependency mutation test.
impact: A regression in `dep add`, `dep add --type=parent-child`, or `dep remove` would miss the new mocked unit coverage even though the bead scope calls out nominal dependency operations.
remediation_hint: Add at least one happy-path dependency mutation test, and preferably one explicit parent-child kind case, asserting both the command line and pending-mutation journal behavior.

`bv_empty_graph_reports_no_next_bead_gracefully` (tests/unit/bv_adapter_test.rs:213-245) and `bv_blocked_graph_reports_all_beads_blocked` (tests/unit/bv_adapter_test.rs:247-280) are byte-for-byte duplicates. Both scripts return the exact same payload `{"message":"No actionable items available"}`, both assert `BvParseError` with identical `raw_output`, `command`, and error-branch structure, and both exercise the same `--robot-next` path. The bead scope explicitly called out these as two distinct scenarios (empty graph vs. fully blocked graph). As written, the second test adds no differential coverage over the first — one test produces the same signal, and running both inflates the count without adding a verifiable invariant.

Fix: either give the two tests genuinely different scripted inputs that distinguish the scenarios (for example, a completely empty JSON object for empty-graph and a payload that explicitly enumerates blocked bead IDs / returns `{"message":"All beads blocked","blocked":[...]}` for the all-blocked case) and adjust the assertions to match each real bv payload shape, or collapse the pair into a single parameterized test covering both call sites with clearly separate inputs. Either approach keeps the bead's requested scenario coverage honest.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
